business.headline: Ola Electric rallies 20% as sales recover: Should you buy at current levels?The stock is now up around 25% in April, marking a sharp reversal after six straight months of losses. The trigger has been a turnaround in March sales, which has helped shift sentiment.

Sensex sinks over 900 points as shaky Iran-US ceasefire drags marketsThe sharp fall comes just a day after Dalal Street logged a strong rally, with the Sensex closing at 76,631.65, down 931.25 points or 1.20%, while the Nifty slipped 222.25 points, or 0.93%, to 23,775.10.

Personal loan on your mind? 7 simple checks to avoid costly mistakesA personal loan can feel like an easy solution when you need quick money. But what looks simple at first can turn expensive if you miss a few key details. A quick check now can save you stress later.

Crude oil prices drop, but is the damage already done?Crude oil has eased after the spike, but disrupted flows, vessel backlogs and persistent risks are already feeding into inflation and growth, with markets still adjusting to the shock.

From SBI to ICICI: A quick look at FD rates across 5 banksWith uncertainty in markets, many investors are turning back to fixed deposits for stability. Here's how FD interest rates stack up across five leading banks right now.

Crude oil nears $100 as Hormuz closes again, fragile ceasefire rattles marketsOil markets are back to trading geopolitics over fundamentals, with every headline on Hormuz and the ceasefire now capable of swinging prices sharply and unsettling global markets within hours.

Confused about ITR forms? Here's how to choose the right one for AY 2026-27Filing your income tax return can feel tricky, especially when you are faced with multiple ITR forms. If you are unsure which one to pick this year, you are not alone. Here's a simple way to understand what suits you best.

Gold steady near Rs 1.5 lakh mark: Should you wait or invest now?There is little movement in gold prices at the moment, but that does not mean the market is settled. Uncertainty around global developments is keeping investors cautious, raising a simple question, i.e., invest now or wait?
Sensex drops 800 points as West Asia tensions hit marketThe BSE Sensex was down 794.65 points, or 1.02%, to 76,768.25 at 10:02 am, while the Nifty 50 dropped 196.55 points, or 0.82%, to 23,800.80.

TCS Q4 today: Will it be a reality check for IT sector after AI shock?Brokerages estimate TCS will report around 1–3% sequential revenue growth, while the broader tier-1 IT pack is likely to post -1% to +1% constant currency growth, pointing to a demand environment that has stabilised but not yet recovered.

Govt cuts airport charges by 25% to support airlines amid West Asia crisisCivil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu said the Centre will cut landing and parking charges by 25% for domestic flights for a period of three months, in a move aimed at easing operational pressure on airlines.

Sensex, Nifty pare some losses as Iran-US ceasefire holdsThe BSE Sensex declined 354.93 points, or 0.46%, to 77,207.97 in early trade. The Nifty 50 slipped 78.45 points, or 0.33%, to 23,918.90, falling below the psychologically important 24,000 level around 9:20 am.

World Bank raises India growth forecast to 6.6% for 2026-27 from 6.3% earlierEven as growth across the region slows, India continues to stand out as the main engine, providing a sense of stability at a time when the broader economic outlook remains uncertain.

Big rally, bigger question: Buy now or wait? | INTERVIEWMarkets rebounded sharply on Wednesday as lower crude prices lifted sentiment. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking discusses if this is a temporary bounce or a trend shift.

Trump warns of 50% tariffs on countries supplying weapons to IranThe statement comes at a time when global geopolitical risks are already high, especially in West Asia. Any move to impose such tariffs could have wider implications for global trade and diplomatic ties.

Borrowing to survive, facing abuse: How India's credit boom is turning stressfulNearly 4 in 10 borrowers report abusive recovery calls, as a new survey highlights rising repayment stress and growing dependence on credit for everyday needs.

Dalal Street adds Rs 17 lakh crore as Iran-US ceasefire boosts marketsInvestor wealth surged by around Rs 17.1 lakh crore in a single session, taking total BSE market capitalisation to Rs 445.8 lakh crore. The S&P BSE Sensex rallied 2,946.32 points to end at 77,562.90, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 873.35 points to close at 23,997.35.

Resilient, safe, strong: RBI assures banking system safe amid recent jittersRBI Governor highlighted that the broader banking system remains stable despite global challenges. He said there are no systemic risks to the profitability or health of banks, even as global uncertainty remains due to the West Asia conflict.

Why did RBI keep repo rate unchanged at 5.25%? Know these 5 reasonsNo change in the repo rate this time, but the move is far from random. The RBI has carefully weighed multiple factors before deciding to hold at 5.25%. Here are five reasons behind the decision.

Sensex jumps 3,000 points: What does this mean for stock market investors?The bulls on Dalal Street are back in control for now, but whether they can hold ground will depend on oil, flows and earnings in the days ahead.

entertainment.headline: Netflix, Prime Video this week: Tu Yaa Main, O'Romeo, Euphoria S3 and more This week's OTT lineup on Netflix and Prime Video brings a mix of thrillers, crime dramas, and returning favourites. From Tu Yaa Main and O'Romeo to Euphoria Season 3, there's something for every kind of viewer.

Ginny Wedss Sunny 2 trailer: Avinash Tiwary-Medha Shankr star in chaotic love story The trailer of Avinash Tiwary and Medha Shankr's Ginny Wedss Sunny 2 shows a mismatched love story. The film is set for a theatrical release on April 24, 2026.

Varanasi plot leaked? What will SS Rajamouli's next step be? With one year left for the release of SS Rajamouli's Varanasi, the film's plot has been leaked online, revealing a time-travel adventure starring Mahesh Babu and Priyanka Chopra. Varanasi is slated for a grand release on April 7, 2027.

Mammootty on who's the hero between him and Mohanlal in Patriot Patriot marks the much-awaited reunion of Mammootty and Mohanlal in a content-driven espionage thriller. In a recent interview, the Bramayugam actor talked about who's the hero in Patriot.

Inside Dhurandhar's massive makeover: Aditya Dhar praises look designer Preetisheel Aditya Dhar honours the exceptional makeup work that brought Dhurandhar's characters to life in his appreciation post for makeup and prosthetics artist Preetisheel Singh.

Rakesh Bedi spices up Adivi Sesh's Dacoit advance booking with a fun video. Watch Advance bookings for Dacoit: Ek Prem Katha have started with a playful promo featuring Rakesh Bedi. Starring Adivi Sesh and Mrunal Thakur, with Anurag Kashyap as the antagonist, the film releases on April 10.

Extraction Season 3 confirmed with Chris Hemsworth and Idris Elba. Report The third instalment of the Extraction franchise is officially set with Chris Hemsworth returning as Tyler Rake. Idris Elba and Golshifteh Farahani will also be reprising their roles.

Nayanthara, Vignesh Shivan visit Tirupati temple ahead of LIK release. Watch Filmmaker Vignesh Shivan and actor Nayanthara offered prayers at Tirupati temple a day before the release of Love Insurance Kompany. The sci-fi romantic entertainer is set for release on April 10.

Everybody Loves Sohrab Handa trailer: Vinay Pathak film has Knives Out vibes The trailer of Everybody Loves Sohrab Handa promises a gripping mix of murder mystery and psychological drama, led by Vinay Pathak.

Silambarasan's Arasan shoot halted amid feud with producer Ishari K Ganesh The shooting of Silambarasan TR's Arasan faced a halt due to a conflict with producer Ishari K Ganesh over a film that didn't materialise. Actor T Rajendar demanded fair treatment and called for resolution amid legal and industry disputes.

Parvathy Thiruvothu: Tired of survivors being questioned in Ranjith case Parvathy Thiruvothu has criticised the system for failing survivors of sexual harassment in the Malayalam film industry. Calling out repeat offences and weak punishments, she stressed the need for real policy changes.

Mom of 2: Anurag Dobhal's wife Ritika has a witty caption for pics of their newborn Ritika Chauhan shared the glimpses of her newborn on April 8, days after welcoming her child with Anurag Dobhal on Ram Navami.

Dog's tail and all: Mukesh Khanna isn't laughing at Samay Raina's Still Alive One joke, and suddenly it's a full Shaktimaan-level showdown. Samay Raina's Still Alive has clearly not amused Mukesh Khanna.

Jenna Ortega almost quit acting before landing You: Didn't know my future Actor Jenna Ortega almost quit acting before landing the role of Ellie Alves in Netflix's popular series You. Her persistence paid off as she went on to star in major films and the Emmy-winning series Wednesday.

Dhurandhar 2 in legal trouble as Trimurti Films sues B62 Studios over Oye Oye song Trimurti Films has taken legal action against Aditya Dhar's B62 Studios for allegedly using the iconic song Oye Oye from the 1989 film Tridev without proper permission in Dhurandhar 2.

SRK, Big B, Kamal Haasan, Hrithik: Extreme makeovers Raaka's Allu Arjun reminds of Allu Arjun's half-human, half-animal look in Raaka highlights a growing trend of actors undergoing bold transformations for their roles. From Kamal Haasan's aged makeover to Shah Rukh Khan's rare bald look, such changes reflect the versatility and commitment of actors in Indian cinema.

Viral pic: Prabhas, Sandeep Reddy Vanga's Dhurandhar 2 outing at Allu Cinemas Superstar Prabhas and director Sandeep Reddy Vanga took a break from filming Spirit to watch Dhurandhar 2 in Hyderabad. The spy action film, starring Ranveer Singh, recently broke box office records.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 21: Rs 100 crore more to beat Pushpa 2, steady in India Dhurandhar 2 continues its record-breaking run but faces its slowest day yet. With Rs 1,653.67 crore worldwide, can it overtake Pushpa 2 in the coming days?

Why Vignesh Shivan is one of Tamil cinema's most exciting new-age directors around Director Vignesh Shivan is all set to release his new film, Love Insurance Kompany. Here's looking at the filmmaker who has carved a niche with innovation in themes and emotional stories despite setbacks.

general news.headline: Pakistan to blame for Lebanon confusion in US-Iran ceasefire? Live UpdatesWhile a ceasefire deal is in place between the US, Israel and Iran, they have been busy in the past 24 hours contradicting each other and themselves in terms of what's actually been agreed to, especially regarding Lebanon. It has put Pakistan, which mediated the deal, at the centre of the storm.

Sunetra Pawar to win Baramati bypolls as Congress pulls out of electionThe Congress's move came after senior NCP (SP) leaders, including Sharad Pawar and Supriya Sule, urged it to pull out of the Baramati bypoll, which is scheduled on April 23.

How Pakistan returned Operation Sindoor favour to Trump in Iran warIn 2025, Pakistan needed a face-saving exit as India pounded its air bases during Operation Sindoor. Then, it desperately reached out to the US. A year later, the US needed an exit in Iran. Pakistan provided it. A classic case of you scratch my back, and I will scratch yours.

7th Pay Commission, women's safety, infiltrators out: PM's 6 guarantees for BengalSpeaking at a rally in Haldia, which goes to polls on April 23, PM Modi promised to establish the rule of law, bring transparency in governance, investigate every case of corruption, throw out infiltrators and implement the 7th Pay Commission if voted to power in West Bengal.

The nuclear stunner India pulled off; US, France invested billions but failedIndia achieved stunning success at the Kalpakkam nuclear plant, which even the US and France failed to. Only Russia has a commercially running Fast Breeder Reactor (FBR). The Kalpakkam FBR will use India's huge reserves of Thorium to power millions of households, and reduce the country's energy imports.

Live: Assam tops turnout at 75.91% till 3 pm; Puducherry at 72.40%, Kerala at 62.71%Assembly Election 2026 Live Updates: Voting is in full swing for the 2026 Assembly elections across Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry, alongside by-elections in Nagaland, Karnataka, and Tripura. Polling began at 7 am and will continue until 6 pm, the Election Commission confirmed. In Assam, 722 candidates are vying for 126 Assembly seats, with the main contest framed as CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's governance record versus the Congress campaign led by Gaurav Gogoi. Kerala's 2.71 crore voters are choosing among 883 candidates across 140 constituencies. While the race involves multiple parties, it primarily pits the ruling Left Democratic Front, led by CM Pinarayi Vijayan, against the opposition United Democratic Front. In Puducherry, roughly 9.5 lakh voters will decide the outcome for 294 candidates across 30 seats. The NDA alliance seeks to retain power, while the INDIA bloc is mounting a strong challenge. Early trends suggest lively voter turnout across all regions.

BJP, Left eye hat-tricks in Assam, Kerala; NDA aims to keep Puducherry Live UpdatesPolling is underway in Assam, Kerala and Puducherry in high-stakes Assembly elections set to test the NDA's hold in Assam and Puducherry, and assess the INDIA bloc's push for a comeback.

US's big move amid Iran war: Eligible men to be auto-registered in military draftThe US has not conducted a draft since the Vietnam War, but rising global tensions, including the ongoing Iran conflict, have renewed focus on preparedness.

Church better take US' side: Trump admin threatened Pope for his anti-war speech?The office of Pope Leo XIV, who is the first American to occupy the Catholic papacy, has found it at odds with the US after he made a speech underlining the importance of diplomacy and the perils of war. Pentagon officials reportedly summoned the Pope's ambassador to the US and demanded the Catholic Church align with the US. JD Vance has however refuted the claims.

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

Iran sent 3 versions of 10‑point proposal, one probably written by ChatGPT: VanceIran on Wednesday claimed a "historic victory" over the US and Israel after US President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire. Tehran also claimed that Washington DC has agreed to its proposals. However, the US has disputed this claim so far.

Who is mediating in mediator Pakistan's war with Afghanistan?While Pakistan is looking to be seen as the mediator that pulled the US and Iran back from the edge, it is itself sitting in China's Urumqi, looking for a way out of its own "open war" with Afghanistan.

I started drinking warm water for weight loss. Here's what no one tells youMany people swear by starting their day with warm water for weight loss. But does it actually work, or is it just another health myth?

Live: PM slams TMC over RG Kar, Sandeshkhali incidents; says women feel unsafeModi West Bengal Rallies Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is all set to address seven major rallies in the state this week, with party insiders hoping that his rallies provide a last-minute fillip to the BJP's campaign. Party insiders add that PM Modi's campaign blitzkrieg could help the BJP convert its strong vote-share performance in the 2024 Lok Sabha election into an electoral breakthrough in West Bengal. The Election Commission has scheduled polling in West Bengal in two phases – April 23 and 29. The counting of votes will take place on May 4.

Samrat Chaudhary likely to be next Bihar CM, Nitish Kumar to resign next weekSources tell India Today that Nitish Kumar is likely to resign as Bihar's Chief Minister on April 14, when the NDA legislative party is expected to meet to elect the next chief minister.

Pune influencer woman cop accused of extorting Rs 7 lakh from school principalThe accused have been identified as Damini squad member Sonali Hinge, who has a significant following on Instagram, Police Sub-Inspector Ajit Bade, and constable Sudam Tayade. A case has been registered against the three.

Dhurandhar 2 in legal trouble as Trimurti Films sues B62 Studios over Oye Oye songTrimurti Films has taken legal action against Aditya Dhar's B62 Studios for allegedly using the iconic song Oye Oye from the 1989 film Tridev without proper permission in Dhurandhar 2.

Live: 200 killed in Israeli strikes in Lebanon, Netanyahu says will keep hitting HezbollahUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East slipped back into instability as Lebanon witnessed some of the heaviest attack by Israel despite a two-week truce with US, with over 100 people killed and around 700 injured. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu made it clear that Lebanon is not part of the agreement; even as the United States and Iran announced a pause in attacks, fresh strikes were reported across Iran, the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain, raising serious doubts over the truce's durability, while US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire — brokered with Pakistan's involvement — includes Tehran allowing safe transit through the Strait of Hormuz, with talks scheduled in Islamabad on Friday to decide the next steps. Iran has claimed victory but stressed that the upcoming negotiations do not signal the end of the war. Trump indicated possible sanctions and tariff relief for Tehran while reiterating that Iran will not be allowed to enrich uranium and warning of steep tariffs on countries supplying weapons to Iran, even as oil prices eased following the announcement, with Brent crude slipping below $95 a barrel. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

Iran issues maps to guide ships around Hormuz mines, state of strait unclearIran has issued new shipping routes in the Strait of Hormuz to counter mine threats, even as uncertainty persists over its reopening and ceasefire terms, with Tehran signalling possible future transit fees.

Man beheads driver over parking dispute in Bihar, victim's family kills himAccording to the police, the deceased driver was identified as Ali Hussain, a resident of Jogbani town in Araria. The roadside vendor killed in retaliation was identified as Ravi Chauhan.

Babar Azam loses cool at journalist over Virat Kohli comparison: Let's end this hereBabar Azam had a heated exchange with a reporter after being asked about comparisons with Virat Kohli during the post-match press conference following Peshawar Zalmi's win over Hyderabad Kingsmen in PSL 2026.

Thoothukudi euphoria and what Vijay needs to do to become MGR, not Sivaji GanesanThousands turned out across Thoothukudi to greet Vijay on Wednesday, signalling strong fan enthusiasm. Analysts cautioned that electoral success will hinge on organisation, voter conversion, and the entrenched welfarism of rival parties.

Not a credible player: Israel envoy doubts Pak's mediation role in Iran warIsrael's Ambassador to India, Reuven Azar, questioned Pakistan's credibility despite US outreach, even as Lebanon reeled from its deadliest day in the current Middle East conflict.

3-6 months left to live: Yuvraj Singh on coming to terms with cancerYuvraj Singh opens up on his battle with cancer, revealing how he ignored symptoms while playing for India before a diagnosis forced him into treatment and a long road to recovery.

sports.headline: 3-6 months left to live: Yuvraj Singh on coming to terms with cancer Yuvraj Singh opens up on his battle with cancer, revealing how he ignored symptoms while playing for India before a diagnosis forced him into treatment and a long road to recovery.

Inspired by Harmanpreet Kaur, Niki Prasad weaves her own World Cup dream In an interview with India Today, Niki Prasad spoke about her ambition to help India win a World Cup, following in the footsteps of Harmanpreet Kaur, who achieved the milestone last year by winning the Women's ODI World Cup.

Ayush Shetty storms into Badminton Asia Championships quarters, HS Prannoy crashes out Ayush Shetty continued his impressive run at the Badminton Asia Championships 2026, defeating Chi Yu Jen to reach the men's singles quarterfinals in Ningbo. Meanwhile, HS Prannoy and PV Sindhu were both knocked out in the Round of 16, ending their campaigns.

Rashid Khan reveals the simple fix to rediscover form after surgery Rashid Khan credits a return to basics for his revival after surgery, delivering a match-winning spell for Gujarat Titans and showing signs of regaining his best form in IPL 2026.

Verstappen's exit talk grows as trusted Red Bull aide in talks for McLaren switch Max Verstappen faces renewed uncertainty at Red Bull Racing as reports suggest his long-time race engineer Gianpiero Lambiase could join McLaren in 2027.

Pak bragging? Babar, Labuschagne release dove before PSL clash after US-Iran ceasefire Pakistan sent out a peace message before the PSL 2026 clash between Hyderabad Kingsmen and Peshawar Zalmi, with captains Babar Azam and Marnus Labuschagne releasing a dove after Islamabad helped mediate the US-Iran ceasefire.

KKR vs LSG weather update: Another washout on the cards at Eden Gardens? KKR vs LSG faces weather concerns after recent rain in Kolkata, but clearer skies are expected to improve chances of a full game at Eden Gardens as Rahane's side hunt their first win of IPL 2026.

Babar Azam loses cool at journalist over Virat Kohli comparison: Let's end this here Babar Azam had a heated exchange with a reporter after being asked about comparisons with Virat Kohli during the post-match press conference following Peshawar Zalmi's win over Hyderabad Kingsmen in PSL 2026.

Not scoring runs, winning hearts: Sanju Samson gifts fan a Rs 30,000 smartphone Sanju Samson gifts a fan a new smartphone during a roadside stop in Kerala, even as he struggles for form with Chennai Super Kings in the IPL, managing just 6, 7 and 9 in his first three innings.

Did Miller miss a trick? When Ravi Shastri's single saved India in the iconic tied Test IPL 2026, DC vs GT: David Miller's refusal to take a single against Gujarat Titans sparked debate, with many drawing parallels to Ravi Shastri's famous single in the 1986 tied Test that at least helped India avoid defeat.

Shubman Gill fined for slow over rate in Delhi Capitals vs Gujarat Titans IPL 2026, DC vs GT: Shubman Gill has been fined for Gujarat Titans slow over rate offence against Delhi Capitals. On Wednesday, the Titans beat the Capitals by one run at the Arun Jaitley Stadium.

PV Sindhu exits in Round of 16 after loss to World No. 2 Wang Zhi YiPV Sindhu's Badminton Asia Championships campaign ended in the Round of 16 after a straight-games loss to World No. 2 Wang Zhi Yi at the Ningbo Olympic Sports Center on Thursday.

When common sense prevailed in PSL: Iftikhar didn't repeat David Miller's mistakeDavid Miller's refusal to take a single proved costly as Delhi Capitals missed out on a golden chance to beat Gujarat Titans. The moment drew comparisons with Iftikhar Ahmed, whose smarter call in a similar situation helped Peshawar Zalmi win.

Can KKR spark a revival vs LSG or will rain spoil party in Kolkata again?KKR are in desperate need for a revival of fortunes in their IPL 2026 campaign and will hope that the game against LSG can change their season around. With rain threat looming large, will Kolkata be able to find the right combination finally.

Rashid Khan may step away from Test cricket, doubtful for India clash in JuneAdvised by doctors to avoid red-ball cricket after back surgery, Rashid Khan is set to prioritise white-ball formats. The Gujarat Titans spinner all but confirmed his withdrawal from the June Test against India in New Chandigarh.

David Miller hero or villain? Costly call is IPL 2026 Play of the Day in DelhiIPL 2026, DC vs GT: In a contest that swung both ways till the very end, David Miller looked set to take Delhi Capitals home after doing the heavy lifting, only for a momentary lapse to undo all his work, as Gujarat Titans edged out a win by the narrowest of margins to get off the mark on Wednesday.

Miller not at fault: Gavaskar defends DC star's no-single call in 1-run lossSunil Gavaskar defended David Miller's call not to take a single during the final over of the DC vs GT game. Miller refused to give Kuldeep Yadav strike in the penultimate ball of the final over as DC lost by 1 run.

Vaishali beats Divya to share lead with Jiner Zhu in Candidates ChessIndia's R Vaishali remained in contention for the Candidates title after beating compatriot Divya Deshmukh in Round 9 on Wednesday, moving into a joint lead with China's Jiner Zhu.

Axar Patel consoles heartbroken Miller as DC lose last-over thriller to GTIPL 2026, DC vs GT: Axar Patel consoled a heartbroken David Miller after the Delhi Capitals fell short by one run against Gujarat Titans in a last-over thriller in IPL 2026 on Wednesday.

IPL 2026: Brave Miller, classy Rahul fall short as GT win thriller to open accountIPL 2026, DC vs GT: KL Rahul's sensational 92 and David Miller's heroic cameo went in vain as Gujarat Titans defended 210 by just a run in a last-over thriller in Delhi. Rashid Khan was the hero for the visiting team, which managed to open its account in the new season on Wednesday.

Billie Jean King Cup: Vaishnavi, Sahaja power India to 3-0 sweep over New ZealandYoung debutant Vaishnavi Adkar and Sahaja Yamalapalli secured decisive victories as India bounced back to blank New Zealand 3-0 in their second Asia/Oceania Group I tie of the Billie Jean King Cup on Wednesday.

Mohammed Shami devising new variations with Bharat Arun to outfox IPL battersMohammed Shami outfoxed Abhishek Sharma with a new delivery he had developed just days earlier while working with bowling coach Bharat Arun, Lucknow Super Giants spin bowling coach Carl Crowe revealed.

Cameron Green set to make his bowling return during KKR vs LSG clash in KolkataCameron Green is all set to make his bowling return in the IPL during the KKR vs LSG clash at the Eden Gardens on Thursday, April 9. Green is yet to bowl in the IPL this season in three matches so far as he has failed to justify his price tag so far.

Doha Diamond League postponed until June due to West Asia tensionsThe opening round of the Diamond League season, scheduled to be held in Doha on May 8, has been postponed to June 19 due to the ongoing conflict in the Gulf, organisers said on Wednesday.

Will Narine and Varun return for LSG clash? KKR sweat over key duo's fitnessKKR have issued an update on Sunil Narine and Varun Chakravarthy after the spin duo missed the last clash against Punjab Kings in Kolkata. Narine and Chakravarthy lead KKR's spin attack this season.

David Warner's alcohol admission surfaces in Sydney drink driving investigationFormer Australian opener David Warner has reportedly told police that he had been drinking with a friend before allegedly being caught for drink-driving in Sydney.

Watch: Medvedev loses cool, smashes racket seven times in heavy loss to BerrettiniWorld No. 10 Daniil Medvedev endured one of the most humiliating defeats of his career, going down 0-6, 0-6 to Matteo Berrettini in the third round of the Monte-Carlo Masters. Medvedev, visibly rattled throughout the contest, smashed his racquet multiple times in frustration.

DC vs GT Highlights: Rahul, Miller heroics in vain as Gujarat win thrillerDelhi Capitals (DC) vs Gujarat Titans (GT) Highlights, IPL 2026: KL Rahul's 92 and heroics from David Miller go in vain as GT secure their first win of the season. Rashid Khan's three-wicket haul changed the contest but Miller brought it close with some fireworks in the end.

Ajinkya Rahane defends batting approach amid growing criticism at KKRAjinkya Rahane has pushed back against criticism of his batting approach, asserting that he has always been an instinctive and attacking player despite questions over his strike rate. The KKR captain highlighted the hard work behind his T20 evolution while acknowledging the team's early-season scrutiny.

Smriti Mandhana-Palash Mucchal patch-up? Viral family meeting video sparks buzzA viral video showing Smriti Mandhana's father with Palak Muchhal and Mithoon has sparked speculation about a possible patch-up between the Indian cricketer and Palash Muchhal after their reported split in 2025.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
